faqs.q: Is emotional regulation the same as suppressing your feelings?
faqs.a: No. Pushing feelings down tends to backfire and can make them stronger. Good regulation usually means feeling emotions and still being able to act with some intention.
Yes. It isn't a fixed trait you either have or don't. It's a learnable skill that tends to grow with practice and age, which is why therapies like DBT teach it directly.

faqs.q: Why does emotional regulation matter for mental health?
faqs.a: Trouble with emotional regulation shows up across many conditions, from depression to anxiety to borderline personality disorder. Building these skills can lower the intensity of emotional storms and give a person steadier ground to stand on.
